principle: Paid or earned currency may expand exploration space but must not raise the final capability ceiling directly.

simulation_batches.description: Additional simulation branches increase search breadth without changing the final best policy ceiling.

risk_insurance.description: Challenge losses may be partially offset by gold, but a minimum sample loss is always retained.

challenge_entry.description: Entering strong player dungeons or system adaptive dungeons requires up-front commitment.
challenge_entry.anti_farming_rule: Repeated attacks on weaker opponents reduce marginal reward.

sample_absorption.description: Imported samples are never free. They carry adaptation noise and can destabilize the receiver.
sample_absorption.blend_formula: P_new = (1 - alpha) * P_old + alpha * P_sample + epsilon

diversity_reward.description: Worlds should reward strategic difference from a collapsing mean.

hard_constraints: Gold cannot replace the minimum guaranteed sample loss in a failed challenge.
Gold cannot directly increase k_inf or any equivalent terminal equilibrium cap.
Samples are the only resource that modifies long-run agent evolution.
